notes: OpenCode parent session uses the `task` tool to spawn ONE foreground subagent (an @explore-style read-only child). The subagent runs in its own child SQLite session whose `parent_id` column equals the parent's id; the irrlicht opencode watcher polls the session table, surfaces the child as transcript_new, and the detector emits parent_linked binding child→parent. The parent's outstanding `task` tool part blocks the parent in working until the child reports back, then the parent settles working→ready. This is the minimal subagents shape (parent_linked_min:1, subagent_transcripts_min:1) — for opencode the daemon-recorded parent_linked count IS the spawn proof (run-cell.sh:447-450), no child .jsonl on disk is required. Delays are generous because (a) the SQLite session-table poll runs on a periodic cadence (less frequent than fswatcher inotify), and (b) the default model is a small/slow local LM Studio model (~30-45s/turn). First phase anchored to "start" UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession row can't steal the birth and cascade same_session_as failures.

text: User prompt drives parent ready → working
Subagent child session row materializes with parent_id set
Detector binds the child → parent (parent_linked); the dashboard subagent count for the parent reads 1

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the child is alive — the parent's outstanding `task` tool part blocks until the child reports back; parent never returns to ready or enters waiting in this window
The child's bash/read work completes, its `task` tool returns, child working → ready (dashboard subagent count drops 1 → 0)
After the child reports back, the parent's step-finish reason=stop fires: parent working → ready. No subagent rows linger past this point — the dashboard shows the parent in ready with zero children.
